chapter_title.en: Chapter 106 – Selling the juice and the wine

text: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) فِي رَجُلٍ كَانَتْ لَهُ عَلَى رَجُلٍ دَرَاهِمُ فَبَاعَ خَمْراً أَوْ خَنَازِيرَ وَ هُوَ يَنْظُرُ فَقَضَاهُ فَقَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ أَمَّا لِلْمُقْتَضِي فَحَلَالٌ وَ أَمَّا لِلْبَائِعِ فَحَرَامٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, form Hammad, from Hareyz, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who had some Dirhams (as a debt) upon a man, so he sold wine, or pigs and he (the creditor) was waiting, so he paid him off. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem with it. As for the creditor, it is Permissible, and as for the seller, so it is Prohibited’.
